questions.question: Briefly describe the Shared Responsibility Model in cloud computing. (2-3 marks)
keyPoints: The model divides security and compliance responsibilities between the cloud provider and the customer.
The provider is responsible for security *of* the cloud (infrastructure, hardware, software, networking).
The customer is responsible for security *in* the cloud (data, platform, application, and identity/access management).

questions.question: Compare and contrast the event-driven, serverless compute services of AWS and Azure. Discuss a typical use case for this architecture. (5-10 marks)
keyPoints: AWS Lambda is the serverless compute service that runs code in response to events.
Azure Functions is the equivalent service on the Microsoft Azure platform.
Both are key enablers of event-driven architecture, executing code without provisioning servers.
Both are charged based on resource consumption and execution time, not reserved capacity.
Contrast: Deep integration with respective ecosystems (Lambda with AWS services, Functions with Azure services).
Contrast: Different scaling behaviors and configuration options (e.g., memory limits, triggers).
Typical Use Case: Image/File processing. A file uploaded to cloud storage (S3/Blob) triggers a function to create a thumbnail, transform the file, or extract metadata.
